name: NONTUBERCULOUS MYCOBACTERIAL LUNG INFECTION

demographics.Location: apical + anterior segments of upper lobes

dominant_finding: centrilobular nodules of varying sizes, usually < 1 cm (= micronodules) in 76-97%
distribution: apical + anterior segments of upper lobes
discriminating_features: chronic fibronodular / fibroproductive apical opacities (indistinguishable from
additional patchy nodular alveolar opacities in ipsi- / contralateral lung in 40-70% ←

general: chronic fibronodular / fibroproductive apical opacities (indistinguishable from
additional patchy nodular alveolar opacities in ipsi- / contralateral lung in 40-70% ←
multiple bilateral centrilobular nodules throughout both lungs in random distribution
HRCT: areas of segmental + subsegmental atelectasis + scarring
CT: multifocal bronchiectasis (79-94%), esp. middle lobe + lingula
centrilobular nodules of varying sizes, usually < 1 cm (= micronodules) in 76-97%

clinical.Rx: multidrug regimen typically prescribed for at least 1 year
